---
description: "The Doss-Gollin Research Group at Rice CEVE develops Bayesian statistical and machine learning tools for climate risk, flood hazard, and community-engaged infrastructure planning."
listing:
    - id: news-listings
      template: _assets/listing-templates/news.ejs
      contents:
          - "posts/*/*.qmd"
          - "posts/*/*.md"
      sort:
          - "date desc"
          - "title desc"
      sort-ui: false
      filter-ui: false
      max-items: 12
      categories: false
      fields: [date, title, description]
      feed: true
      date-format: "MMM YYYY"

    - id: featured-papers
      template: _assets/listing-templates/papers.ejs
      sort: false
      max-items: 8
      fields: [image, title, details]
      contents:
          - bibliography/publications/article/pollack_transparency_2026.qmd
          - bibliography/publications/article/pollack_unsafe_2025.qmd
          - bibliography/publications/article/liu_generative_2025.qmd
          - bibliography/publications/article/lu_spatiotemporal_2025.qmd
          - bibliography/publications/conference/kazadi_icml_2024.qmd
          - bibliography/publications/article/doss-gollin_subjective_2023.qmd
          - bibliography/publications/article/doss-gollin_txtreme_2021.qmd

format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.home-page}

::: {.home-hero}

::: {.grid .align-items-center}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5 .home-hero-video}
<iframe
    src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/gI2I8Cq-14g"
    title="Overview of the Doss-Gollin Research Group"
    allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share"
    allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-7 .home-hero-text}
The **Doss-Gollin Research Group** at [Rice CEVE](https://ceve.rice.edu) builds probabilistic hazard models that enable climate risk analysis and bottom-up decision support for complex infrastructure systems, with a focus on urban flooding.
We integrate Earth science, AI, and decision analysis to characterize nonstationary hazards, quantify their impacts on the built environment, and design adaptive risk-management strategies.
We co-develop these tools with structural engineers, water managers, industry, and community organizations.
:::

:::

:::

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-6 .home-news-col}

::: {.section-header-with-link}
### News

[all news →](news.qmd){.see-all-link}
:::

::: {#news-listings}
:::

:::

::: {.g-col-lg-1}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-5 .home-papers-col}

::: {.section-header-with-link}
### Featured Papers

[all publications →](publications.qmd){.see-all-link}
:::

::: {#featured-papers}
:::

:::

:::

:::
